Subjective:

75 year old male presents today for Assessment:
Medical concerns:

Pt with 2 hospitalizations this year due to fall with subsequent head trauma. Pt had seizures post head trauma
and started on keppra. Pt then with weakness and AMS was taken from dialysis back to the ED was noted to
have a large subdural hematoma that was then evacuated and sent to SNF. Pt discharged from SNF followed up
with neurosurgery with noted post op infection at the site of the surgery. Pt had brain wash, IV antibiotics and
advised to stay on keppra indefinitely. PT was recently seen by neurology and has follow up with neurosurgery.

Pt with ESRD on Dialysis MWF. Per pt states that there have not been any changes to his medications. Pt
currently states that he is doing and feeling well.

Pt with DM type2 currently using insulin without any secondary side effects. Per pt states that when he last had
blood drawn was told that his sugars were doing well. Pt denies any hypoglycemic or hyperglycemic events.

Pt with charcots foot(right) has been seen by podiatry and has follow up with bionics--was recently measured for
shoes.

Pt with chronic shoulder pain and back pain. Pt has had imaging done and referral to sports medicine for further
evaluation and possible joint injection. Discussed with patient physical therapy, however, per pt states that it
does not really help. Discussed topical patches to see if that will help with pain.

PT with depression has been referred out to mental health, pt currently on medication and denies any SI/HI, no
10/08/2025 2:39:19 PM -0700 FAXCOM PAGE & OF 21

auditory or visual hallucinations. Pt currently denies any worsening of symptoms.

Pt with dementia on medication, denies any worsening of symptoms. Per wife, states that patient is the same.
Wife has noted that pt is stubborn and does not listen to what she or son tell the patient that he should be doing.
Discussed with patient and he agrees that he is stubborn and will try to listen to what his wife tells him.

Pt with BPH and doing well with medications. Pt denies any urgency or frequency with urination. Pt still has
urine output, has been taking his medication as prescribed.

Pt with IBS, has been referred out to GI for further evaluation. Will need to reschedule his colonoscopy. Pt
currently denies any blood in the stool, denies any diarrhea at this time. Pt has no other complaints.

Pt currently denies any chest pain, no heart palpitations, no dizziness or vertigo. Pt denies any cough, no
shortness of breath or difficulty in breathing. Pt denies any dizziness, no headaches, no blurry vision or double
vision. Pt denies any GI issues at this time. Pt denies any recent ED visits or hospitalizations.

Other Data/Comments:

Subjective:

Mr Richards is a 74-year-old male with a past medical history significant for cerebrovascular accident (2017),
referred for close monitoring of cirrhosis identified on imaging. Imaging showed a nodular liver and borderline
to low platelet count.

Encounter date with UCSD hepatology was on 9/9/2025 with below care plan

Objective:

Assessment/Plan:

Assessment:

Compensated Cirrhosis-

Likely alcohol-associated.

MELD score: 6 (as of 3/4/2025)
MELD-Na score: 6 (as of 3/4/2025)

Laboratory Values Used for MELD Calculation (3/4/2025):
11/25/2025 4:21:28 PM -0800 FAXCOM PAGE 7 OF 16

e Serum Creatinine: 0.96 mg/dL (minimum of | mg/dL used for calculation)
e Serum Sodium: 139 mmol/L (maximum of 137 mmol/L used)

e Serum Albumin: 3.7 g/dL (maximum of 3.5 g/dL used)

e INR: 1.0
e Age at listing (hypothetical): 73 years
e Sex: Male

Additional Findings:

Diagnosis of cirrhosis is based on imaging showing a nodular liver and a history of low platelet count (initially
<150,000, though recent labs in March 2025 were normal).

The FIB-4 score is 2.02 (as of 3/4/2025).

An MRI with MR elastography has been ordered but not yet performed.

A prior CT scan from January 2025 demonstrated cirrhosis with signs of portal hypertension.
An abdominal ultrasound with elastography is planned for the next imaging study.

The history of thrombocytopenia and portal hypertension further supports the diagnosis.

Etiology:

e
Most likely secondary to alcohol use history, but serologic work-up demonstrated positive autoimmune
hepatitis (AIH) markers (see serologic work-up above).
e

Liver enzymes currently normal. Liver biopsy to be considered if lrver enzymes worsen. Comprehensive serologic
work-up will continue to rule out other liver diseases.

Plan:

General:
